title: BFS — Shortest Path in Grid
description: Given an n×m grid where '.' is walkable and '#' is a wall, find the shortest path length from S to E. Each step moves to an adjacent cell (4-directional).
inputSpec: First line: n and m. Then n lines of m characters each. S=start, E=end.
outputSpec: The minimum number of steps from S to E, or -1 if no path exists.

botCodes.code:
from collections import deque
n,m=map(int,input().split())
grid=[input() for _ in range(n)]
sr=sc=er=ec=0
for i in range(n):
    for j in range(m):
        if grid[i][j]=='S':sr,sc=i,j
        if grid[i][j]=='E':er,ec=i,j
dist=[[-1]*m for _ in range(n)]
dist[sr][sc]=0
q=deque([(sr,sc)])
while q:
    r,c=q.popleft()
    for dr,dc in[(-1,0),(1,0),(0,-1),(0,1)]:
        nr,nc=r+dr,c+dc
        if 0<=nr<n and 0<=nc<m and grid[nr][nc]!='#' and dist[nr][nc]==-1:
            dist[nr][nc]=dist[r][c]+1
            q.append((nr,nc))
print(dist[er][ec])
